subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Acquire and preprocess open-source models and evaluation datasets, complete the software and hardware environment configuration required for the proposed solution

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with frontier and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python, able to use basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Resources required for environment configuration

Check whether the hardware of the running environment meets the following requirements: GPU preferably NVIDIA A10 or above (optional), GPU with VRAM ≥ 16GB (optional), CPU ≥ 8 cores, RAM ≥ 16GB, operating system Linux (Ubuntu 20.04+).
Please list a checklist for self-inspection:

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Environment configuration resources ready
- Output Description: Server configured with GPU driver (optional), Python environment, Node environment, meeting hardware and system requirements for model deployment

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Install and configure Claude Code via Node

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Install and Configure Claude Code
- Input Description: Install and configure Claude Code based on Node environment
# Configure environment variables (using ~/.bashrc as example, similar for ~/.zshrc, etc.)
# Start confirmation command, enter 'claude' to launch CLI. Configuration is complete when you receive corresponding responses without errors after inputting any text

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Available Claude Code Service
- Output Description: Automatically invoke related MCP services through Claude Code to complete enterprise-level demo video production

subsections.title: Step Definition

content: - Output Description: Build and start SSE protocol ffmpeg-mcp service

steps.title: Video Compression Ratio Calculator MCP Service Build and Start

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Build custom MCP service for video compression ratio calculation (named cus-tool-video_compression_calculator) to calculate compression duration allocation for multiple videos, ensuring the total duration after compression does not exceed the target duration

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Configure and start song-generation service to support text-to-instrumental-music generation (named bgm.wav)
Note: This step requires GPU and has certain configuration complexity, you can also use online products like https://suno.com/

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with frontier and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python, able to use basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: song-generation Service Configuration and Start
- Input Description: Install and start related web services by installing Python dependencies
# Pull docker image (ensure network connectivity), and start service entering container

Note: After starting, visit http://IP:8081 in browser, enter text in the left text box, then click the button below to generate song/instrumental music (you can play and download the corresponding audio file after generation)

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Name: Usable song-generation Web Service
- Output Description: Configure and start gradio song-generation web service

steps.title: Generate Demo Video Using Claude Code

subsections.title: Step Definition
content: Use Claude Code to automatically invoke the previous MCP services to generate demo video

subsections.title: Participants
content: 1. Proficient in various chain-of-thought strategies, familiar with frontier and popular open/closed-source large model resources, with personal usage experience, summaries, and insights

2. Proficient in NLP classic deep learning models (such as Transformer series, LLaMA series, GLM series, etc.) and related resources (websites, libraries, blogs, etc.); master at least one common deep learning development framework, such as PyTorch; maintain continuous attention and interest in the working principles and latest news of large-scale generative language models (LLMs) after GPT-3.5

3. Proficient in Python, able to use basic regular expressions and command-line scripts; familiar with NLP basic concepts and classic tasks (classification, matching, sequence labeling, generation, etc.); able to proficiently use common NLP open-source libraries (HanLP, LTP, Jieba, etc.)
4. Proactive attitude, organized communication, curiosity and self-motivation

subsections.title: Input
content: - Input Name: Generate Demo Video Using Claude Code
- Input Description: Interact with Claude Code to confirm input information to generate demo video
First register the MCP services built and started in previous steps in Claude Code
claude mcp add -s user -t sse asr-funasr http://127.0.0.1:4005/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse tts-paddle_speech http://127.0.0.1:4006/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse cus-tool-video_compression_calculator http://127.0.0.1:4007/sse
claude mcp add -s user -t sse video-ffmpeg http://127.0.0.1:4008/sse
Pass in existing video materials and video_editing_skill.md
│ ├── number.title.suffix # Different functional screen recording video files (required)
# video_editing_skill.md content as follows (see source file for complete content):
Start Claude Code in project root directory to generate demo video
# Start claude with higher permissions, please ensure no other files in project root except files and video_editing_skill.md, and forbid using dangerous commands like "rm -rf *"
> Generate demo video based on video materials and video_editing_skill.md

Note: There will be options for video duration etc. requiring user confirmation, then the final demo video will be automatically generated (generation quality depends on the LLM, ASR, TTS models used, and LLM has certain randomness)

subsections.title: Output
content: - Output Description: Final demo video automatically edited by Claude Code
